title: AN ACT INCREASING THE BURIAL ASSISTANCE FOR VETERANS FROM TEN THOUSAND PESOS (P10,000.00) TO TWENTY THOUSAND PESOS (P20,000.00), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6948, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS ГўВЂВњAN ACT STANDARDIZING AND UPGRADING THE BENEFITS FOR MILITARY VETERANS AND THEIR DEPENDENTSГўВЂВќ AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 694; Manila Bulletin, Phil. Star, December 1, 2014; 111 OG No. 2, 192 (January 12, 2015)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10649, November 27, 2014 ]

AN ACT INCREASING THE BURIAL ASSISTANCE FOR VETERANS FROM TEN THOUSAND PESOS (P10,000.00) TO TWENTY THOUSAND PESOS (P20,000.00), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6948, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS ГўВЂВњAN ACT STANDARDIZING AND UPGRADING THE BENEFITS FOR MILITARY VETERANS AND THEIR DEPENDENTSГўВЂВќ AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. Section 20 of Republic Act No. 6948, as amended, is hereby further amended to read as follows:

ГўВЂВњSEC. 20.

Burial Assistance.

ГўВЂВ“ Unless the person who

defrayed the expenses for the funeral of a deceased veteran is entitled

to a similar benefit from the United States Government, he or she shall

be given Twenty thousand pesos (P20.000.00) as burial assistance upon

application therefor in due form which shall be filed within two (2)

years from the death of the veteran concerned.ГўВЂВќ

SEC. 2.

Appropriations. ГўВЂВ“

The amount necessary for the

initial implementation of this Act shall be charged against the current

yearГўВЂВ™s appropriations for burial expenses of the Philippine Veterans

Affairs Office (PVAO). Thereafter, the amount necessary for the

continued implementation shall be included in the annual General

Appropriations Act.

SEC. 3.

Effectivity. ГўВЂВ“

This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in two (2) national newspapers of general circulation.

Approved,

(SGD.) FRANKLIN M. DRILON

President of the Senate

(SGD.) FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was

finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on May 19,

2014 and September 15, 2014, respectively.

(SGD.) OSCAR G. YABES

Secretary of the Senate

(SGD.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved: NOV 27 2014

BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
